summary: Matched Kirby fighter function `ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1` to 100% by introducing a local prototype for `ftAnim_80070458` with the argument types needed by this callsite and by reshaping nearby control flow in `ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0`. The patch was small and localized to `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c`.
what_changed: Added a local declaration `void ftAnim_80070458_proto(Fighter* fp, struct KirbyFV_x44_t*, int);` near the top of `ftKb_Init.c`.
Changed `ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1` to call `ftAnim_80070458_proto(fp, &fp->fv.kb.x44, arg1)` instead of `ftAnim_80070458(fp, &fp->fv.kb.x44, arg1)`.
Renamed parameters in `ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0` from generic `arg0`, `arg1`, `arg2` to `kind`, `out1`, and `out2`.
Rewrote `ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0` from nested conditionals into early returns plus a `goto case14` label, preserving the behavior for `kind == 14` and for the `11 <= kind < 14` range.
smart_moves: Used a callsite-specific prototype to make the compiler treat `ftAnim_80070458` with the exact pointer type needed for Kirby fighter variables, avoiding broader header churn in this small matching PR.
The `goto case14` shape in `ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0` suggests the author matched the original compiler's branch layout rather than only writing clean source-level equivalent logic.
Kept the change tightly scoped to a single Kirby init source file, reducing risk while solving the targeted 100% match.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn matches, local function prototypes can be a practical tactic when the existing declaration does not produce the same codegen at a specific callsite.
Equivalent C logic is not always sufficient for matching; branch ordering, early returns, and labels may be necessary to reproduce the compiler's emitted control flow.
Improving parameter names such as `kind`, `out1`, and `out2` can clarify intent even in matching-focused patches, as long as it does not disturb codegen.
When dealing with fighter-specific union or extension fields like `fp->fv.kb.x44`, exact struct pointer types can matter for function signatures and matching.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter init symbols use the `ftKb_Init_` prefix.
The matched callback pair function is named `ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1`, retaining `Unk` where behavior or semantic naming is not yet established.
The demo callback helper remains `ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0`, but its parameters were made more descriptive: `kind`, `out1`, and `out2`.
A temporary/local prototype was named with a `_proto` suffix: `ftAnim_80070458_proto`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced a local typed prototype for `ftAnim_80070458` to alter callsite type information without renaming or globally changing the underlying function.
Reordered conditional checks in `ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0` to test `kind == 14` first, then reject `kind >= 14`, then reject `kind < 11`.
Used explicit early `return` statements and a label to shape the generated branch structure.
Preserved write order in the `kind == 14` case as `*out2 = 17; *out1 = 17;`, which may be important for matching.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states: `Match ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1 (100%)`.
follow_up_queries: Search for other uses of `ftAnim_80070458_proto` to see whether the local prototype pattern was reused or later cleaned up.
Check whether `ftAnim_80070458` eventually received a canonical header declaration matching `Fighter*` and `struct KirbyFV_x44_t*`.
Review nearby Kirby callback-pair functions for similar branch-shaping or prototype issues.
Investigate the semantics of `ftKb_Init_UnkCallbackPairs0_1` and `ftKb_Init_UnkDemoCallbacks0` to replace `Unk` names when behavior is understood.
